cataloging_subject.label: Scottish Alliterative Poems in Riming Stanzas (Amours five-poem text selection)
cataloging_subject.scope_note: Editor-defined selection consisting of the title leaf, five poem texts on printed pp. 1-187, and four parallel Pistill appendix streams on pp. 190-245; neither one medieval work nor the complete volume.

catalog_summary.title: Scottish Alliterative Poems in Riming Stanzas (Amours five-poem text selection)
catalog_summary.author: Richard Holland and anonymous authors
genres: Advice to princes and governance counsel

titles.value: Scottish Alliterative Poems in Riming Stanzas (Amours five-poem text selection)

assertion.notes: Boundary-explicit project title, not a historical title variant.

titles.value: Scottish Alliterative Poems in Riming Stanzas
The Knightly Tale of Golagros and Gawane
The Awntyrs off Arthure at the Terne Wathelyne

agents.name: François Joseph Amours
notes: Editor, transcriber, and collator of the 1897 edition; 1841-1910.

agents.name: Richard Holland
notes: Known author of The Buke of the Howlat.

agents.name: Huchown
notes: Historical attribution label; its application here is disputed.

agents.name: Clerk of Tranent
notes: Historical attribution associated with Golagros and sometimes Rauf; not preferred.
Richard Holland is known for Howlat; the other four preferred responsibilities are anonymous.
No blanket Huchown authorship is assigned.

date_statements.display: late fourteenth to early fifteenth century
notes: A 1420s proposal and broader dating remain unresolved.
Darnaway/Moray and Douglas patronage are associations, not proven composition place.

language_statements.label: English
notes: Scoped to editorial/title matter, not medieval works.

form_statements.description: All five intellectual works are alliterative verse in rhyming stanzas; the title leaf is editorial framing.

genre_statements.label: Advice to princes and governance counsel

subject_statements.label: Social order, hospitality, and the king in disguise
Mortality, revenant warning, and land conflict

summaries.value: Five alliterative stanzaic poems edited by Amours: Golagros and Gawane, The Buke of the Howlat, Rauf Coilyear, The Awntyrs off Arthure, and The Pistill of Susan. Parallel streams represent two Awntyrs and five Pistill witnesses.
Includes the standalone title leaf and printed pp. 1-245 as encoded. Excludes front matter, pp. 188-189, and pp. 249-481. Five works are counted once; ten streams are representation layers.

extent.description: Representation extent of the encoded five-work, ten-stream selection; excludes 45 apparatus lines in nineteen Q1 blocks and is not a canonical scholarly line total.

content_parts.label: Complete encoded selection
content_parts.summary: Title leaf plus five poem texts and four parallel Pistill appendix streams.

content_parts.label: Selection title leaf
content_parts.summary: Standalone editorial title leaf represented at the start of the XML.

content_parts.label: Five poem texts
content_parts.summary: Wrapper containing five intellectual works and ten represented witness-version streams.

content_parts.label: The Knightly Tale of Golagros and Gawane
content_parts.summary: Anonymous Older Scots Arthurian romance from the unique 1508 print.
notes: 1,365 is an XML representation metric; DIMEV gives 1,362.

content_parts.label: The Buke of the Howlat
content_parts.summary: Richard Holland's Older Scots beast fable and political allegory.

content_parts.label: Rauf Coilyear
content_parts.summary: Anonymous Older Scots Charlemagne romance from the unique 1572 print.
notes: 974 is an XML representation metric; DIMEV gives 975.

content_parts.label: The Awntyrs off Arthure at the Terne Wathelyne
content_parts.summary: Anonymous Middle English Arthurian romance in parallel Douce and Thornton streams.
notes: Metrics sum two versions; they do not assert 1,430 unique lines.

content_parts.label: The Pistill of Susan
content_parts.summary: Anonymous Middle English biblical paraphrase in five witness streams.
notes: Metrics sum five versions; they do not assert 1,720 unique lines.

content_parts.label: Awntyrs: Thornton version
content_parts.summary: Parallel edited stream from Lincoln Cathedral MS 91.

content_parts.label: Pistill: Vernon main version
content_parts.summary: Main edited stream from the Vernon manuscript.

content_parts.label: Pistill: Cotton version
content_parts.summary: Incomplete parallel stream from Cotton Caligula A.II.
notes: The first eight stanzas are absent.

related_works.title: The Knightly Tale of Golagros and Gawane

Amours edition title, responsibility, publication, selection framing, descriptions, and texts.
Amours source notes and qualified transcription and collation practice.
DIMEV work records, dates, identifiers, witnesses, line counts, and attribution cautions.
Current Golagros description and attribution caution.
Current Rauf description and romance context.
Current Awntyrs description and dating debate.
Current Pistill description and five-witness context.
Awntyrs romance, witness, date, and regional evidence.
Richard Holland authorship and c. 1448 Howlat context.

open_questions.question: Which proposed date within the late-fourteenth to 1420s range best fits Awntyrs?
Can Awntyrs be identified securely with Huchown's Gret Gest of Arthure?
next_steps: Do not treat the identification as settled.

open_questions.question: Does Wyntoun's Huchown attribution securely identify the author of Pistill?
Do Golagros and Rauf share an author, or support historical Huchown or Clerk of Tranent attributions?
What are the exact shelfmarks, physical unity, split history, and image availability of the Cambridge and Dundee Howlat fragment reports?
next_steps: Ask both repositories to reconcile their holdings.

open_questions.question: Does DIMEV's Sloane 1098 item with a different incipit represent a source witness relevant here?
next_steps: Compare the text and incipit before promotion.

open_questions.question: How should the f. 213r versus f. 213v Asloan start-locus disagreement be reconciled?
next_steps: Preserve both reports and use the visually inspected f. 213v start.

open_questions.question: What is the exact Douce 324 Awntyrs locus, and can a complete whole-codex surrogate be obtained?
next_steps: Request images and reconcile the locus reports.

open_questions.question: Do Amours's working transcripts survive, and which supplier handled each Pistill witness?
next_steps: Search archives; do not invent person-specific edges.

open_questions.question: Can complete public target sequences be found for Rauf, Lincoln, Lambeth, Additional, Cotton, or the split Howlat fragment?
next_steps: Recheck catalogs and preserve request routes.

open_questions.question: Can the complete 610-image U-M carrier bundle be obtained through an authorized route?
next_steps: Ask U-M; do not bypass HTTP 403.

open_questions.question: Why do XML and DIMEV totals differ for Golagros and Rauf?
Is the 1966 Johnson carrier a page-for-page photographic facsimile of Amours 1897?
How should Primo shelfmark RB.x.006(2) be reconciled with the cached SRU record that omits it?

notes: The immediate carrier is Johnson Reprint Corp. [1966]; the cached 1897 scan is a comparison surrogate.
English is scoped to editorial/title matter; medieval languages are Older Scots and Middle English.
Nested version parts have item_count null so parallel streams do not inflate the five-work count.
No blanket Huchown attribution is assigned.
